programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Former nanny Amber wants to have beautiful babies on a farm, but does her dashing date have the film star looks shes after? A magician and a pole dancer are looking for love too.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, Amelia Dimoldenberg, Sarah Beeny and Maxine Peake take cupcakes to a new level, before recreating memorable romantic dates in biscuit form.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George takes on a 16th century listed Cotswold cottage with some nasty surprises. Theres also a Kent oast house needing a super cool bedroom for an eight-year-old, where he tries to make a disco ball and neon lights work with exposed oak beams.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers help out at the 191st Durham Regatta, discover the secrets of Durham Castle and explore the exotic wonders of Durham University Botanic Garden.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In France, craft brewery owners Simon and Rebecca sink their savings into a new taproom. Can Natasha and Giles keep their Spanish restaurant-bar afloat?

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners deliver a wow-some villa for Mark and Julie. In Alicante, the Rodriguezes give brothers-in-law Graham and Norman some food for thought.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her expertise to find Neil and Linda a rustic holiday home around the picturesque town of Antequera in a traditional Andalusian village in southern Spain.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Rachel and Robin dream of buying a holiday home in Alicantes Jalon Valley, with the hope of relocating there in the future. Can Ben Hillman find their perfect place for £250k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Louisa and Roger rush to finish a suite for their first wedding. Debbie and Dave transform their disused barn into a craft retreat. Alison and Zion tackle mismatched windows.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on their favourite working from home transformations, from a cluttered terrace in Northampton to a three-bed semi in Waterbeach.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition is in Hull and first to host is 29-year-old welfare team leader Jennie. Jennie is honouring Hulls fishing history with her under-the-sea theme and hopes to win the prize catch.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the second night of this new competition in Hull, and the turn of 28-year-old wrestling promoter and novice cook, James to host. James is hoping to get his hands on the prize with a Mexican themed night that includes staging a full blown wrestling match as his evenings entertainment.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition is in Hull and third to host is 47-year-old wedding stationery entrepreneur, Katrena. Vegetarian Katrena loves nothing more than a bit of glitz and glamour, and shes hoping to wow her guests with a showy cruise ship themed night to bag the thousand pound prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Hull and fourth to host is 41-year-old super-confident mum of three, Natalie. Unimpressed by the food so far, Natalie reckons good quality produce can easily bag her the thousand pound prize. But can she win her guests over?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fifth and final night of the weeks competition in Hull and retirement community manager George is hoping his menu of simple tasty favourites will win him the prize. Having heavily criticised the food on the past four nights, expectations about his menu are running high.

programme.title: Greggs vs McDonalds: The Fast Feud
programme.desc: How did Greggs steal McDonalds fast food crown and how is McDonalds fighting back? Dani Dyer explores this extraordinary fast food feud.

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: Christy and Matt pour £1m into a seven-bed house in serious need of TLC. Heather and Dave take on converted stables that they bought when they were on holiday!

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the weeks biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Worlds Tallest Man: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Fifteen years after becoming a global phenomenon, The Worlds Tallest Man: The Next Chapter follows Sultan Kosen as he faces the true cost of life as the worlds tallest man -- exploring whether fame and love delivered the future he dreamed of.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, Amelia Dimoldenberg, Sarah Beeny and Maxine Peake take cupcakes to a new level, before recreating memorable romantic dates in biscuit form.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: A 90-year-old sings the Frank Sinatra classic that got him his voice back after a stroke. A 15-year-old sings about growing up Black in Britain. And father and son perform a lullaby.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance attends a high-speed collision in South Ayrshire and a minibus crash by Loch Lomond. And a man has a severe cut to his arm after an attempt to chop wood with a circular saw went horrifically wrong.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Essex and Herts Air Ambulance race to help teenager Jack who has a major brain injury after colliding with a car in Hemel Hempstead.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim is hot on the tail of a speeding motorcyclist, Jon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan and dog handler Barry is brought to book by a wannabe legal eagle.

programme.title: The Outcast
programme.desc: Jet Cosgrave has been swindled out of his fathers ranch by a wicked uncle. He returns eight years later with a bunch of gunslingers headed by the psychopathic Duke Rankin, and sets about plotting his revenge. Distractions, in the comely shapes of naïve ranch girl Judy Polsen and Alice Austin, his uncles fiancee, cannot deter Jet from the final showdown, but not before the two sides have already shed a lot of blood in dirt.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: The interceptors have to drop everything when a speeding 4x4 rushes past them. The Stanway team pursues a man with a machete on the run in a public park.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully investigate the deaths of soldiers, thought to be suicides, from a Haitian refugee camp in North Carolina.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: Conor Woodman is in Jerusalem to expose the corrupt market traders, crafty pick-pockets and ruthless con men who prey on the holy citys unsuspecting tourists.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Steve tackles the mud with high voltage headaches. Sludges mountain mission goes downhill, while Camerons journey gets hotter and harder.

programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nations roads.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Traffic Cop Stuarts first task is to attend the scene of an accident in which a motorbike rider has been catapulted from his bike on a bend. Officers also attend the site of a horrific head on collision involving a transit van and an HGV.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A look back at the highlights of the series, including the units efforts to catch a stolen 4x4 that tries to run a police Evo off the road. Elsewhere, officers have a trick up their sleeve when a speeding driver attempts to outrun nine cop cars.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A cyclist gives deputies the runaround; a boyfriend saves his girlfriend from jail on her birthday.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: An alleged burglar claims his sack full of money is a gift from his grandma; the discovery of a pink bandana leads deputies to question a suspects story; a deputy attempts to get a man to cool off after a fight with his wife.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Agent Mulder is discovered unconscious and suffering from hypothermia, but how did he come to be in this state?

programme.desc: Andrew is on track with reclaiming his fitness, joining fitness classes with the Costa fitness expert, taking extended walks and changing his lunch options.

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, cops look out for criminals and illegal drivers moving around under the cover of darkness. After a spike in catalytic converter thefts, a stinger is used to catch some thieves.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The cops deal with a major drugs bust in North Yorkshire, a wanted man linked to an alleged assault on a woman, and a gang of suspected poachers who are caught driving an untaxed van.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Over a Bank Holiday, near Scarborough, a high-speed pursuit ensues after a driver fails to stop. In the Dales, an operation is underway to crack down on motorists flouting the law.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A reckless driver gives Sergeant Steve the slip in bad weather but whatever their wiles on the ground, the sly suspect cannot escape the eye in the sky. Ben has a run-in with an argumentative drink driver who fails three breath tests.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A white van man makes a break for it after ploughing into a parked car, a suspect is not keen on wearing a set of cuffs, and the driver of a stolen car is brought to a halt thanks to some railings.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors chase down two separate runaways using wildly different tactics. Plus, dog handler Duncan assists armed response officers in Leeds city centre following reports of a man with a pistol.

programme.title: Shoplifters and Scammers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Documentary charting methods used to thwart criminals targeting shops, beginning with a private crimes team employed to catch shoplifters in Londons West End.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash and Jo search for Tane. David is onto Newman. Brax and Rickys quiet family life is under threat.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash reports back to Eden as his search for Tane continues. He and Jo are canvassing every beach near Exmouth.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The doctors of Balham Park Surgery treat patients with heart concerns, including a man whose father has a rare genetic condition, a heart attack victim who requires serious lifestyle changes, and a girl who experiences heart palpitations when she has a panic attack.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going behind the scenes at a GP surgery in Lewisham.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Following specialist emergency response teams on their most extreme medical call outs, embedded with the Critical Care and Air Ambulance Response cars.

programme.desc: Three patients with worrying abdominal problems consult the Balham Park doctors.

programme.desc: In 1967, as it edged towards its centenary, a much-loved social and cultural institution was consigned to history. Following 95 years of emotional highs and lows, Glasgows Third Lanark Athletic Club was bankrupt, and the gates to Cathkin Park were closed.

programme.title: Fàsach na h-Eòrpa
programme.desc: For 9,000 years, the European wild sheep could not be driven from its original territory in Cyprus and Sardinia. However, Poachers almost wiped it out, but the wild ancestor of todays domestic sheep, the mouflon, is a survival artist.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds, and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unlocks the ancient roots of the Northumberland village of Warkworth. With the help of locals, he discovers clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest and how the invaders laid the foundations of a planned community, still visible to this day.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across England, Robbie crosses an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon and gets stuck in a lock in central Birmingham.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: A financier travelling from Istanbul to Paris is found dead, stabbed a dozen times, and it is up to celebrated Belgian detective Hercule Poirot to solve the crime. He painstakingly interviews those in the same railway car and through an elaborate re-creation aims to find out just who carried out the deed.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career.

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Screen adaptation of Oscar Wildes comic masterpiece. Set in 1890s England, a comedy of mistaken identity ensues when two gentlemen friends use the same alias to pursue the women they love.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Ninety million miles away from us is the power that shapes our world - the Sun. Using the latest satellite images, and the expertise of Britains leading solar scientists, Kate Humble and Helen Czerski reveal the inner workings of our very own star, and the influence its mysterious cycles of activity have on our planet.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: As transatlantic liners brought Americas super-rich to the region, art and celebrity became integrally intertwined as cultural gurus and multi-millionaires all partied on the beach. In an era of sunshine and bathing, of cinema and fast cars, of the Ballet Russes and Monte Carlo casinos, Richard E Grant discovers the extraordinary output of what became briefly the worlds creative hub.

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary tracing how human understanding of the jet stream - a ribbon of fast moving air high in the atmosphere - has grown. It has been responsible for bewildering effect on bomber pilots during the Second World War.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Forest Elephants: Rumbles in the Jungle
programme.desc: Andrea Turkalo has been studying the complex social lives and unique vocalisations of the Central Africas Forest Elephants, getting to know over 4,000 intimately. As new acoustic research sheds light on the many mysteries that still surround forest elephant society. Will these endangered elephants finally speak out and tell Andrea what it is they need to survive?

programme.desc: The show comes from Dover Castle in Kent. Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to take off to auction. programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: On the face of it, humans are a bit breakable. We have these hard skeletons that can crack if you have a tumble. But if we were indestructible, like a superhero, then wed never have to worry about ending up in plaster! So whats the best way? First we try removing the problem - its our bones that break, so lets get rid of them altogether. Unfortunately, this doesnt work very well. Without our bones wed basically be blobs and unable to move anywhere.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Orwell and Omni must locate Onom before a cute creature destroys HQ.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: BB and Cyborg invite Starfire to join in their fun as long as she avoids dreaded uncle jokes. The Killer Moth offers a reward for Silkies return, but will Silkier be happier away from the Titans?

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Roy and Ult return to the Lost Mayo Maze for a rematch with Lucario.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job as a baker, Frog realises he can get free treats!

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers are working on cleaning all the habitats, and they discover how much they can learn from the animals themselves, as they are natures best clean-up crew.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Rattus has an idea that might make him millions, he is going to launch a streaming service. However, to make Ratify a success, he is going to need some great songs from history.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: With the help of A-Troupe, Richelle sets in motion a plan she hopes will rid the Next Step of Miss Angela.

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall looks at a shark with superpowers. The epaulette shark is a small shark that lives in shallow, tidal waters. They are able to survive out of water for a long period of time, and Steve heads into his lab to how they manage it.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Lemon with his laugh-out-loud stand-up act about all things green (and yellow).

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is busy building a tower as tall as he is with his blocks, and is upset when Coco arrives and hijacks the blocks so that she can make a rainbow pattern with them instead. When even Charlie wants to play with the blocks, it is clear a new game needs to be found so that they can all play together.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, Dipsy is King. Tinky Winky delivers Tubby Custard and Tubby Toast to King Dipsy, whatever King Dipsy wants. Luckily for Tinky Winky they soon swap places.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee insists on wearing his favourite Action Ape outfit all afternoon, no matter what games hes playing. But when the outfit gets mucky, Tee is heartbroken. This is a job for Action Mo!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato and Broccoli go on a pirate adventure with Captain Pak Choi, but they dont realise she intends on plundering Evil Peas freezer and stealing all her shiny things.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are planting seeds at Ammas when Coco arrives with her new skipping rope. Coco teaches them how to skip, with Sula picking it up quicker than Bing. Just when Bing thinks he has succeeded, he hurts his leg on the watering can. Bing cant understand why he is not as good as Sula, but Coco shows Bing that, with some help, he can learn to skip too.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A shark on the loose in the streets of Swoppiton? Mojo and Bo are on a fishing trip to find out more! But the hungry shark may not be quite as hungry-sharky as they think.

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered what goes on at the Squirrel Club when the sun goes down?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Happy has always loved the water, even though it makes a mess. But while swimming around in his paddling pool at the playhouse, Duggee reminds the Squirrels that there was a time when Happy feared water. On Happys first day, his mum drops him off and he is excited. He goes to play with Norrie and Roly and has loads of fun baking, playing with toys and jumping over puddles, but Happy cant do it.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad agrees to play Sheepdog with Bluey and Bingo to stop them from disturbing Mum who is taking a well-earned rest, but looking after a sheep is harder work than they expected.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Dad always take out the bins. Over a few weeks, Bingo tells Dad about a new kid at kindy who is mean to her and Mum offers some good advice.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters face their toughest mission yet: organising a birthday party for Ubercorn without him finding out! Luckily, when Glitch and Ubercorn were cadets together, they made a map of all the secret passages around the Academy.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Sleepy Hedgehog
programme.desc: Peter volunteers to take Mrs Tiggy-Winkle home in time for her winter hibernation, but little does he know how much trouble a sleepy hedgehog can be!

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe, Bethan, Topsy and Sparky head to the local Cafe, but Topsy isnt happy about being the only one who has to wait outside.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts encounter a Snapping Shrimp, a small creature that uses its claws to make a big and stunning noise.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One, Two, Three and Four are off on a castle-exploring adventure, with a little help from the friends they make along the way. Learn how to make numbers up to 10 by adding with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Z has left the others in charge while he has a nap, but before long disaster strikes and Alphaland needs colouring in again. This episode has fun with simple letter blends and the words rub, stop, pots, dots and cans.

programme.title: Nick Copes Popcast
programme.desc: Nick and Norman are identifying the birds singing in the forest and hear a strange sound. Its their broken cuckoo clock. The birds try to help them fix it by searching for the missing key.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift visit the Fortress of Fun, where Kingsley and Queenie play a fun game of Wavey Wavey Snap using some giant, silly gloves.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing drives his toy car around the breakfast things on the table and accidentally spills Krispos all over the kitchen floor. He helps Flop to clear them up with the aid of the voo-voo cleaner but neither of them notices when Bings car gets sucked up along with the Krispos.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `strawberry, and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. Grow strawberries, pick them and enjoy eating yummy ones. There are skipping and space strawberries, and Dee even does the samba with one. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dees friend finally succeeds in saying todays word.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu and Elodie fear theyve broken a treasured toy of Elodies mum by accident.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Brian sets up a treasure hunt with riddles for the Piggies, but Wilf hears about it and wants to get the treasure and keep it for himself.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a boy tie his shoelace at a bus stop and get the idea to connect his shoelaces to the shoes of the other passengers. When the bus arrives they all stand up, only to fall in a heap!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble needs to give Tumble Ted a bubble bath after a jammy mishap at breakfast. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends have lots of fun blowing bubbles.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Its a windy day and the Teletubbies collect lots of leaves that have blown off the trees. The leaves go everywhere in Home Dome, so the Noo-noo cleans up.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Remi, Otis, George, Holly, DJ, Luke and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyones bags are cardboard tubes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate cardboard tubes inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby. The popular song `Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a shopping centre with her friend George and plays a game of I Spy.

programme.title: Whats in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Herb farmer Jekka introduces the Fact Pack to mint plants. They discover how mint grows, what makes it taste and smell so fresh, and how mint can be used in many different ways.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca and Murphy are trying to come up with a new move to perfect their competition dance routine. They take a break for an ice-cold monster shake and find time to create a bottle penguin. The Mini Monsters demonstrate how to make everlasting sandcastles, and Rebeccas aunt calls from space - where she has made some alien friends. The Recycling Express arrives with empty tissue boxes, inspiring a mega monster make - a funny pineapple puppet.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Stuck in a cave during heavy rain, Ginger and the Peas find fun playing indoors for once.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad chides Bluey for not minding her manners at the dinner table, but when Bluey gets a hold of a magical asparagus stick, she proves that everyone has a wild side.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels cant figure out if fish have noses, Duggee shows them his library. But when the Squirrels realise no-one is visiting it, they have an idea. A mobile library. The Squirrels set off with Duggee. Along the way, they help Tino find his creativity, King Tiger figure out his favourite ice cream flavour and Worm find her love of fantasy. The Squirrels learn just how helpful and informative a library can be.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Take a plunge into the ideas pool with Tino the Artistic Mouse. Everything you see can be art.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through town to water the flowers. On the way, they spot some horses, pretend to be ducks, look at colours in the sweet shop window and walk like signposts!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Eden and Ava enjoy a family training session led by Dad who has lots of exercises for them! Can they complete 20 jumping jacks and who will win the relay race?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Scarlet is cooking a colourful French vegetarian ratatouille, which her mum has taught her to make. The recipe is full of lovely fresh vegetables that Scarlet chops up and cooks for her friends. Scarlets friends taste one of the main ingredients before they come round, but arent too sure about courgettes at first. Hopefully Scarlets recipe can change their minds. She serves it with french bread and creates a beautiful floral table setting.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is having fun outdoors in a playground, learning how we keep swinging on a swing, and how gravity helps us slide down a slide.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks is excited to visit the allotments again with her owner Charli to see how tall their sunflowers have grown. Tinks is a disability assistance dog for Charli and uses her super fetch skills and energy to help Charli every day.

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet a sea anemone to see how it feeds with the help of another animal. Then, they go to see an amazing pitcher plant, which feeds on bugs and insects to survive, and discover the unusual wild connection between the animal and the plant.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle learns that the summer holidays will soon be over and Evie will be going to school. Determined not to be left alone, Waffle hatches a plan to keep the family at home.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba help Otto to find his playful little siblings.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The Potatoes explore science in this new wave music video. From bugs to electricity, science is all around us.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: When rapper Bushy T visits the garden, his hip-hop beats may help poor Stripes master Yukees skipping rope and join in the fun.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie and Triggs help a new dog settle in and meet some new friends at the dog park.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It is John and Nigels 6-year anniversary together, and John is planning a big surprise party at their home, but Nigel is always in. He is also stickler for routine and does everything precisely.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.sub-title: Youre Five Ones and Im Five Tens
programme.desc: Fiftys song shows Five the patterns that make them similar.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock I accidentally creates a flood of ink. How can she clear it up?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a zoo, where they meet pandas, lemurs and elephants. Meanwhile, Tumble Cottage ends up full of animals when all the teddies have play dates.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies take turns to go up and down on the Dup Dup. Sometimes they go fast and sometimes they go slow - and even the Noo-noo has a go.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how ducks play such an important role in the environment. They find out that ducks can carry seeds on their bodies so that plants find new places to grow and help keep nature in balance by eating slugs and snails. On the way, they discover that ducks recycle their own feathers in their nests to keep their eggs and babies warm and can help keep ponds and wetlands healthy!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Will Jaz get all her tasks done in time to be able to perform her egg dance with her Dad?

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies very own motorbike rider, Grace, shows off three amazing machines that work in sustainable forests! Grace helps drive a tree harvester, shows off a tree-planting machine and then gathers logs with a tree-forwarding machine.

programme.title: Dodges Pup School
programme.desc: The pups are getting arty and crafty, creating masterpieces for their class show-and-tell. Whilst all the pups are busy working on their creations, Dodge struggles to find the inspiration he needs to start.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Its Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Good Idea Puppy decides that they should start the activities with the ice slide.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Octonauts and the Mountain River Cave
programme.desc: Shellington, Kwazii and Ryla rescue a lost tiger while exploring a river cave; the Octonauts clear the ash around an armadillos home after a fire.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Treehouse Rescue
programme.desc: When Peter is forced to reveal the location of the secret treehouse to Flopsy and Mopsy, the girls decide to hang out in Peters den - just as Old Brown launches a tree-top attack!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly has brought his big brothers action hero figure to the Clubhouse. His name is Jet Rocket. He is known for zooming through space, smashing and crashing things and most importantly, saving the world.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit is very serious about using sunscreen and even asks Vida to apply it to his shell. But when he gets distracted while having fun in the sun, a sunburn appears and he needs Vidas help again.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato and Chilli are playing a computer game when Evil Pea takes it for herself.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy and Spicy have to cover their ears when Sunny learns to play the guitar.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Farmer Jackson was just trying to grow some giant tomatoes, but he wound up with a giant pig! Now Mojo and Bo have to get Felix back to normal size before Swoppiton gets stomped.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad agrees to play Sheepdog with Bluey and Bingo to stop them from disturbing Mum who is taking a well-earned rest, but looking after a sheep is harder work than they expected.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Pancake is persuaded by Nugget to have a massive birthday party, but when the bash grows increasingly over the top, he finds his own unique way to handle the pressure.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Dad always take out the bins. Over a few weeks, Bingo tells Dad about a new kid at kindy who is mean to her and Mum offers some good advice.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Dr Ollie forgets who he asked to play the wizard in his play! Avas memory-jogging machine helps him recall, but things get confusing at the nursery.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: An old friend of Gramps, Mr Willow, brings along his two dogs to play with Waffle. Evie feels a bit left out until Waffle reassures her. The two new dogs escape into Mrs Hobbss garden, so Waffle has to save the day.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi promises her dads she will go straight to bed while Aunty T babysits. But when Aunty T suggests night-time ice cream, Rafi makes a wish and her bed magically flies them out into Manchester! At first, its exciting, but soon Rafi becomes sleepy and wants to go home. Suddenly, the wish runs out and the bed drops onto a tree. Luckily, Aunty T uses her umbrella to magic them safely back. Rafi realises bedtime is bedtime for a reason.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Spring: Its Time for Some Science
programme.desc: Its been raining, so JoJo wants to get outside and splash in the puddles. Isaiah arrives for his play date with JoJo, but Isaiah wants to stay inside and read his science books. Gran Gran suggests they compromise. Gran Gran has a plan: they can use the puddles outside to do a science experiment inside. They use timers and the sun to track the edge of the puddle and find out how long it takes for a puddle to disappear.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Its Ducks birthday, so Sarah decides to bake a cake to celebrate.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Iron Chicken goes off on a trip to see the Wols. Tiny is missing her so the Soup Dragon comes up with a way to cheer Tiny up. She will pretend to be the Iron Chicken!

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo plays the flute beautifully. BaBa is disheartened when they struggle to play like MeMo. MeMo helps BaBa find a way to make some lovely music and join in on the recorder.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy is the best at kicking the ball. Igglepiggle finds it harder so she kindly teaches him. At last Igglepiggle does the best kick of all - all around the garden.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.sub-title: Eilish McColgan: The Fastest Tortoise in Town
programme.desc: Eilish McColgan reads a charming, funny story about courage, self-belief and confidence.

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is toasting Chandler and Monicas engagement, but after Chandler fails to come up with the goods in the bedroom, he begins to worry about the consequences. Elsewhere, Monica thinks Rachel is trying to steal her glory when she catches her kissing Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monicas dream of a fairytale wedding fizzles out after she learns what her parents have done with her wedding fund. Meanwhile, Rachel fumes when a teasing Joey stumbles across her juicy late-night reading material.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandlers desperate effort to bond with Monicas dad by playing racquetball backfires when he accidentally gets too up-close-and-personal. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel must hire an assistant and agonises over whether to hire an efficient, experienced woman, or sexy young Tag Jones. Ignoring her friends advice, she hires Tag and soon regrets it. To make matters worse, Tag is attracted to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Engagement Picture
programme.desc: While taking an engagement picture for himself and Monica, Chandler cant stop pulling a strange face, Rachel enlists Joey to take Tag out for a night on the town, and Phoebe and Ross date a couple in the middle of a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Nap Partners

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss Library Book
programme.desc: Ross is elated that the university library contains his doctoral dissertation, but he is shocked to discover that it is located in a remote corner frequently used by students having sex. Elsewhere, the tables are turned on Joey when Erin is the one to just want sex and not a relationship.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Doesnt Like Dogs
programme.desc: Joey may have ruined Rachels chances with Tag when he opens his mouth during Thanksgiving dinner, and Phoebe reveals that she has been secretly keeping a cute dog in her room for a few days while its owner is away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy
programme.desc: Rachel is dating her assistant, Tag, but they must keep it a secret at work because of the companys anti-dating policy. Her friends warn her that the relationship will only cause her trouble, but just for fun, she becomes more brazen about it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbours cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Theyre Up All Night

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Ant Anstead Master Mechanic
programme.desc: Ants ambitious Alfa build is in the home stretch, but his new baby could come any day now. Ant gets good news about his plan to unveil the car at the US Grand Prix, but a million critical details must still be sorted out.

programme.title: James May and The Dull Mens Club

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Aubrey and Jonathan tried out rural living for a few years, but soon figured out that maintaining a 10-acre property complete with horses is a full-time job. They are on the hunt for a new property, but first they must spruce up their farmhouse.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, Gemma and her `wee-bald man husband Jim serve up a very healthy menu that caters for pescatarians as well carnivores. Second to host are Leeann and her husband Micheal, and finally, Matt and Paul lay on some unique dance routines.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: South African-born Nigel, and his wife Nikki, kick off the competition in South Wales. Day two sees Darren and Simon attempt to woo their guests with a flavour of their first-class lifestyle, and finally its Neil and Carlys chance to impress.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Matthew has just three weeks, £14,000 and the help of his best man to make his dreams of a Roman Empire-themed wedding come true. But will his fiancee Zoe-Jade approve?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: James and Hollie are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for James to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding alone. He and Hollie have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know nothing until the big day. Will James find the perfect venue and get the dress right?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Gary Findley, the self-described redneck CEO of Restoration 1 goes under cover to ensure that there are no flaws in the company playbook that would negatively impact the franchises.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Paul Damico, the former president and CEO of Round Table Pizza goes undercover to learn ways to expand the longstanding West Coast pizza franchise eastward, and attract a younger clientele.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasnt taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesnt slow down hes in danger of collapse.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Oakhurst, New Jersey, to help Mike Farber, the overwhelmed owner of Mike and Nellies restaurant, who lost his way when Nellie, his father and business partner, died. Opened in 1996, the restaurant was a lifelong dream for the father and son. Alone at the helm, and still mourning the loss of his father, Mike has taken his eye off the ball and the restaurant has gone to pot.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Kim and Pete are looking for a spacious family-friendly home where they can keep an eye on their three boys.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.sub-title: The Final: Did I Meet These Potatoes Before?
programme.desc: Noble knights Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican battle to win this elaborate joust to become the 14th Taskmaster champion.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: The show gets an 80s makeover as part of the `Channel 4 80s Night. Lee Mack and David ODoherty compete against Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the famous words and numbers quiz, as `8 Out of 10 Cats team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by celebrity guests. In this first show Jason Manford joins Sean, while Rhod Gilbert teams up with Jon. `Countdown regular Susie Dent is in the `Dictionary Corner with guest Danny Dyer.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Jen has found love, and is giving a dinner party with her new bloke Peter and six of their single friends. However, when the three male invitees drop out at the last moment, Jen is faced with two options: cancel, or bring along Moss, Roy and Richmond.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: When a bad bra makes Jen lose concentration in a board meeting, Moss overcomes his fear of ladies underwear and invents a bra that will never, ever go bad. Could this be their ticket out of Reynholm Industries?

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Pash performs a clean-up for pole dancer Joe, Sketch sees to Hollis belly-button buddy, and Uzzi creates a magical tattoo for Gary, the son of Paul Daniels.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.sub-title: The Final: Did I Meet These Potatoes Before?
programme.desc: Noble knights Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican battle to win this elaborate joust to become the 14th Taskmaster champion.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Club Med Europe Africa Middle East CEO Xavier Mufraggi goes undercover at his holiday parks.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.sub-title: Letting Go Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: With one kid already in college, Adina and Trey are looking to downsize. They are eager to sell fast before a subdivision floods the neighbourhood with a thousand brand new homes, but their outdated property is making a lousy first impression.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oasis tribute singer Degsy and his wife Jackie kick things off with a Manchester-themed menu. Second night hosts Jo and Vikki serve an Indian-themed menu, and the final night sees Chris and Bex stepping up to the plate.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newlyweds Jen and Callum kick off the competition in Aberdeen with an American-themed menu. Day two sees Debbie and Shaun opting for a Scottish themed menu, and finally its the turn of fun-loving couple Sam and Jamie to host.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Drunk Bride
programme.desc: Nick and Laila are madly in love and have been engaged for two years. It would have taken them years to save up for Lailas dream wedding day, instead they will receive £12,000 towards their big day and a chance for Nick to prove himself to the woman he loves. They have agreed that Nick will organise the wedding alone and the bride wont know a single thing about it until the big day.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Tony and Pippa are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for Tony to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding. He and Pippa have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know absolutely nothing until the big day. Three weeks apart will push their relationship to the limit. Will his best men give him the best advice?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Cocos Bakery, steps off from the corporate ladder to go undercover on the front line.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Austin, Texas, home to struggling restaurant El Greco, a business in danger of imminent collapse. El Greco is owned by mother and son Athina and Jake, and claims to be an authentic Greek restaurant. Much to Gordons disgust, the duo have secretly recruited `Chef Mike the microwave to help them out on a daily basis.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michons, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future thats about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: With three girls under the age of 10, Kylah and Jon have been too busy to move, but they desperately need a bigger house and the Property Brothers step in with a plan. Yet, when the house hunt hits a snag, Kylah and Jon are faced with a choice.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Birmingham, Talisa and her boyfriend Luke kick off the competition with a traditional Italian menu. They are joined by Kelly and Rob, and retiree Denise and her partner Peter.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by celebrity guests Miles Jupp and Rhod Gilbert. Plus, Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in `Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Douglas hires Jen as his new PA, giving Moss and Roy free reign in the office. How will they cope without her, and how will she cope with Douglass unwelcome advances?

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: While boss Douglas does his best to run Reynholm Industries into the ground, Jen has to deal with a builder from hell. Meanwhile, Roy loses 20 quid, and Moss learns how to stand up to ruffians.

programme.title: The Night House
programme.desc: Reeling from the unexpected death of her husband, Beth (Rebecca Hall) is left alone in the lakeside home he built for her. She tries as best she can to keep together-but then the dreams come. Disturbing visions of a presence in the house call to her, beckoning with a ghostly allure. But the harsh light of day washes away any proof of a haunting. Against the advice of her friends, she begins digging into his belongings, yearning for answers.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone
programme.desc: A lone rider arrives in a small town and meets a young boy who, while awaiting trial for the murder of the local bully, fears he may be killed by his victims family. As he becomes involved in the morass of shifting partnerships that dominate the community, the rider vows to fight against the three corrupt brothers who are intent on taking the law into their own violent hands.

programme.title: The Violent Men
programme.desc: A soldier turned pacifist, recently returned from a bloody war, is forced into a confrontation with a bullying cattle baron. The disabled cattleman uses violence and scare tactics to push out smaller ranchers and farmers. He is so involved in his evil pursuits, he fails to notice the love affair being carried on by his wife and brother.

programme.title: Sea Wife
programme.desc: During the evacuation of Singapore, refugees are loaded onto a ship that is later torpedoed by the Japanese. Four survivors, three males and one female, find themselves stuck together in a lifeboat. Back in Britain, one of the males searches for the whereabouts of the female survivor.

programme.title: Hell and High Water
programme.desc: Espionage thriller about a plot to make America appear responsible for a communist nuclear strike on North Korea. A submarine commander leads a secret mission to the Arctic to check reports that Russia is assembling a nuclear arsenal on a remote island. The film is so virulently anti-communist that even a British critic called it a "disgrace and an insult".

programme.title: Midway
programme.desc: On June 4, 1942, the Japanese navy plans a strike against American ships in the Pacific. For the next three days, the U.S. Navy and a squad of brave fighter pilots engage the enemy in one of the most important and decisive battles of World War II.

programme.title: Quadrophenia
programme.desc: A rebellious teen vents his anger with the Mods and the Rockers in 1964 London. Based on the album by the Who.

programme.title: This Is England
programme.desc: A fatherless boy growing up in Thatchers Britain joins a gang of skinheads and falls under the spell of an ex-con with radical nationalistic sympathies.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Set several centuries in the future, the abandoned Alita is found in the scrapyard of Iron City by Ido, a compassionate cyber-doctor who takes the unconscious cyborg Alita to his clinic. When Alita awakens, she has no memory of who she is, nor does she have any recognition of the world she finds herself in. As Alita learns to navigate her new life and the treacherous streets of Iron City, Ido tries to shield her from her mysterious past.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul returns to Cyprus, the island he called home for six years. He visits its capital city of Nicosia to show viewers the baking culture he knows intimately and loves dearly.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Seafood king Ricks off to Galicia in Spain to sample some rare shellfish. Plus recipes for hake with peppers and saffron, and clams in a marinara sauce.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: This programme looks at the preparation of a spicy shark vindaloo and the making of an authentic dhal with lentils. Plus a visit to Chesapeake Bay in America, where freshwater oysters and crab cakes are the order of the day.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree goes over the top taking some of her favourite recipes to the Nth degree, including mac and cheese stuffed peppers, grilled French toast, fried chicken pizza and funnel cake sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree mourns the loss of her mother-in-law by preparing a variety of dishes for the family based on her favourite meals and classic recipes.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: American Classics

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: A new series kicks off with Cake Week, with challenges includinga a retro classic Technical and a Showstopper birthday cake taking the bakers dozen back to their childhoods.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Everyday
programme.desc: Mary travels to Scotland to enjoy the best of their everyday larder with recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, and shares the joy of venison cooked on a BBQ with chef Tom Kitchin.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his UK tour, James is joined in glorious Devon by his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. James visits an extraordinary mushroom farm situated in a bungalow, and makes a tasty steak and mushroom dish. Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to make a cheese platter fit for a king. The day ends with a trip to the Art Deco splendour of Burgh Island.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by pal and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, and together they set out to explore the delights of Jersey aboard mini motor bikes. James samples the delights of one of Jerseys most famous exports, the Jersey Royal potato, and he uses them to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark Jordan gets a lesson in oyster shucking, and James discovers an island speciality: Jersey black butter, which he uses to create a delicious sweet treat.

programme.desc: Seafood king Ricks off to Galicia in Spain to sample some rare shellfish. Plus recipes for hake with peppers and saffron, and clams in a marinara sauce.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once theyve reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri digs into all kinds of killer grub. In Lansing, an out-of-this-world pizza joint, where the pie comes with a surprise, and in Wilmington, standout sandwiches bring a taste of Philly to the South.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri grabs some off-the-hook seafood and savoury sensations. In Lansing, a food truck dishes out shrimp burgers and a unique potato popper. In Tucson, a Bosnian restaurant fires up legit Eastern European specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri dives into joints where you can literally grab all kinds of grub, starting with a Chicago-style spot in Tucson firing up deep-dish pie and twisted-up breadsticks.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri grabs some barbecue and Southern comfort. In Seattle, a funky joint smokes up ribs and dishes out brisket chilli. In Wilmington, a breakfast-brunch spot gets dirty with their biscuits and their meatloaf.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Hogfish and BBQ shrimp; 15 dozen raw oysters in under an hour.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: A rack of baby back ribs; juicy pulled pork; a six-pound BBQ sandwich with fries.

programme.desc: Buddy makes a canine friend when he is asked to make a cake for an animal shelter. He also challenges his father-in-law to a pizza-making contest.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernseys wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaires yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: Ottolenghis Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: Yotam Ottolenghis Mediterranean journey concludes in the country where he grew up: Israel. In Tel Aviv, he explores Israels freshest produce, samples seafood with a twist and cooks alongside cutting-edge chefs. Yotam explores the national obsession with hummus and begins by making the popular Mediterranean dish shakshuka, where eggs are gently poached in a spicy sauce of tomatoes, onion and hot chilli.

programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isnt afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UKs first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis `Worth Their Weight in Gold is dedicated to the staff who saved his brothers life and Craigs dish `The Missed Celebration pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Warings two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course, the chefs return to the kitchen to cook their fish dishes. James is making his elevated take on a fried chicken takeaway as a tribute to the NHS staff who care for his sister. Shauna is also taking inspiration from a personal connection with a tribute to staff from a facility in her hometown of Londonderry. Tommy is hoping to impress with a dish dedicated to the discovery of DNA.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two courses down its time for the chefs to cook their main courses. Its the battle of beef with each using the same meat in their innovative creations. Tommy also meets the first baby ever born on the NHS.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course and the final chance for the chefs to impress. After such a tightly fought battle all week, each is hoping to deliver a technically perfect dessert. Tommy and James are both using passion fruit in their complex dishes while Shauna is hoping to use her pastry chef training to score full marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Marys Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything theyve got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that theyll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeares first theatre in Londons Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeares rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.
